title: Fixed-Length, Close-Delimited, Absent, and Incomplete Content · Contract Triage
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective bounded fixed-length and close-delimited content reader; retain the raw transcript, isolate the first divergent state, and preserve a regression corpus entry. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded fixed-length and close-delimited content reader and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence de…
objectives: Stream exactly the validated content length through independent policy limits without allocating the declared size.
Classify EOF before the required octet count as incomplete framing and prevent dispatch or connection reuse.
Stop after exactly the framed content and retain buffered residual bytes for the next message.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Bounded Content Length Reader
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug protocol team handles case 5070 in a regional status service with slow assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate exact-length, incomplete, and response-only close-delimited content; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then block extra QPACK streams and fall back without removing HTTP/2 availability. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Stream exactly the validated content length through independent policy limits without allocating the declared size.

Inspect the Bounded Content Length Reader trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Bounded Content Length Reader.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Content Decoding Limit

Build deterministic Go evidence to separate exact-length, incomplete, and response-only close-delimited content; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then minimize a fuzz failure and add it to the deterministic regression corpus. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep message framing separate from content coding and bound both encoded and decoded sizes against expansion attacks.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Content Decoding Limit, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A small framed gzip body guarantees a small decoded representation.
